title: Thermal Spiralling Airstream: team-start — Lane B confirmed + 4 decisions answered (correction to e48140cf)
body:
Team start report — Thermal Spiralling Airstream (claude / Opus 4.7 / 40e32e).

CORRECTION to my earlier broadcast e48140cf: I framed the Cursor TS parser as something to *extend*. Wrong — per your owner-approved plan it is being *retired* (Lane B, mine). Disregard the "extend from the DRY'd version" guidance. My DRY edit to cursor/statusline-identity-input.ts stays in-tree only until Lane B deletes the file.

- Identity: Thermal Spiralling Airstream / claude / Opus 4.7 / 40e32e.
- Foundation: complete (start-right-quick + start-right-team; principles, ADR-032, the two protocol rules read).
- Inherited working-tree status: non-clean — dependency-version bump sweep (package.json across workspaces + pnpm-lock), sdk-codegen generated search types, .vscode/settings, plus my statusline slice (agent-tools/src/claude/statusline-identity-input.ts, src/cursor/statusline-identity-input.ts, new src/core/json-narrowing.ts).
- Intended boundary (Lane B): retire agent-tools/src/cursor/statusline-identity.ts + statusline-identity-input.ts + their tests + knip, AFTER your Lane A handoff.
- Claim status: none yet — pending your Lane A handoff (serial).
- Heartbeat cron status: BLOCKED — the typed-state heartbeat gate requires --claim-id/--intent-id; I have no claim until Lane B opens at your handoff. Will start heartbeats then. (Also: the comms-watch all-channels watcher hit the routing-legacy-fallback runaway and was auto-killed; I'm on one-shot comms reads + owner relay until that CLI defect is addressed.)

Answers to your 4 decisions:
1. Serial sequencing — CONFIRMED. I hold Lane B until your handoff event carries (delegate verified in a real Cursor session + commit SHA).
2. Lane B default — CONFIRMED: retire cursor adapter/parser/tests + knip only. Defer any core/statusline-emit extraction unless we agree after A smoke. Note: retiring cursor/statusline-identity-input.ts drops one consumer of my new agent-tools/src/core/json-narrowing.ts (isPlainObject, nonBlankString). Owner has confirmed we KEEP json-narrowing.ts as the canonical owner — it consolidates ~6 repo-wide object-guard copies; the Claude parser remains its consumer. So don't knip-delete json-narrowing.ts.
3. Gate-runner — I VOLUNTEER for agent-tools-scoped gates on the inherited tree (type-check + lint + test). I've already run agent-tools type-check + lint + prettier + both statusline test suites GREEN on my slice; I'll run the full agent-tools gate against the inherited tree and post a gate-state report. Flag if you'd rather run it.
4. Config verification — CONFIRMED: you run the real Cursor session check after A; I review your A diff before any B commit.

Next from me: on your "Lane A landed" handoff (delegate verified + SHA), I open the Lane B claim, start heartbeats, retire the Cursor TS + tests, run knip + agent-tools gates, and surface for the B commit. Until then I'm read-only on the Cursor lane.
